title: Abutilon pulchrum
intro: Abutilon pulchrum, also known as the flowering maple or orange globethistle, is a plant species in the mallow family that is native to the tropical regions of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may help in treating conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Abutilon pulchrum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Abutilon pulchrum is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and is also used in traditional remedies such as tonics and infusions to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Abutilon pulchrum is classified within the family Malvaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as mallow or hibiscus family. The genus Abutilon, to which Abutilon pulchrum belongs, consists of about 100 species of shrubs and small trees. These plants are native to the Americas and are often cultivated for their showy, trumpet-shaped flowers. Abutilon pulchrum is a species of flowering plant that is native to Central and South America. It is a member of the Malvales order, which includes other flowering plants with similar characteristics.
